00:16:07	Doug's OtterPilot:	Hi, I'm an AI assistant helping Doug Mather take notes for this meeting. Follow along the transcript here:  https://otter.ai/u/NSEHwRHgeovHEill6LDOLxl89Pw?utm_source=va_chat_link_1

You'll also be able to see screenshots of key moments, add highlights, comments, or action items to anything being said, and get an automatic summary after the meeting.
00:20:02	Ian Johnson:	Q4 - can a barrier be people ?
00:20:40	Johannes Fessler:	Reacted to "Q4 - can a barrier b..." with ✅
00:20:42	Mark - Psybersafe.com:	Replying to "Q4 - can a barrier b..."

00:21:02	Cialdini Institute:	Hi all, just want to let you know that in a bit we’re heading into break out rooms. Therefore I’ll be removing the AI notetakers, so we only have real participants in the rooms :-)
00:22:24	Cialdini Institute:	Replying to "Q4 - can a barrier b..."

Great question Ian, and yes people can definitely be a barrier. For example if one person of a group doesn’t want to agree with a proposal, that could be a barrier. Does that make sense?
